briefing.opening: Hello everyone. I will now begin the climate briefing for July 10, 2026. Today, we will provide a comprehensive overview of important news, ranging from the direct impacts of climate change spreading globally, to domestic and international efforts for carbon neutrality, and the expansion of renewable energy.

sections.title: 🔴 Climate Change Crisis and Call for Immediate Action
sections.content: Recently, climate change has posed a direct threat to food prices and human health. Coffee prices have surged, with 'e-coffee' prices skyrocketing by 28% in just two weeks [6, 24, 25]. New publications highlight how the climate crisis is harming human health, and disease issues are intensifying, such as the outbreak of Legionnaires' disease in New York being linked to climate change [29, 165]. Furthermore, ecosystem disruption is severe, with increasing threats from pests and diseases due to climate change, and sharp declines in tropical mountain wildlife and the UK woodcock population [40, 158, 171]. Heatwaves sweeping across Europe and North America are challenging the 'no-air-conditioning' culture and increasing harmful ozone concentrations [47, 160, 172], while also causing casualties, such as a surge in water-related and safety accidents along Jeju's coast within two days [151]. Amid criticism that the Intergovernmental Panel on Climate Change (IPCC) underestimated the severity of summer [162], analyses suggesting the Iran war exacerbates the climate crisis, and news of an impending landmark climate change lawsuit ruling by a Paris court further underscore the urgent need for action [154, 170].

sections.title: 🇰🇷 Domestic Climate Change Research and Adaptation Efforts
sections.content: South Korea continues its efforts to understand and adapt to the complex aspects of climate change. The icebreaker Araon has embarked on its 17th Arctic expedition, conducting crucial scientific missions over 83 days of Arctic Ocean exploration, including tracking climate change, observing the seabed environment, and surveying Arctic sea routes [3, 4, 5, 8, 12, 19, 43]. Climate change is also bringing changes to domestic dining tables, ushering in an era of 'domestically sourced fresh tuna,' with high-grade bluefin tuna previously exported to Japan now distributed for local consumption. Adaptation efforts in the agricultural sector are also active, such as Yeongju City in Gyeongbuk applying climate change-responsive cultivation techniques to strengthen the competitiveness of 'Yeongju Apples' [2, 14, 15, 33]. Furthermore, the Korea Forest Service is strengthening forest cooperation with Mongolia, including desertification prevention, to respond to climate change, thereby building international cooperative relationships [1, 16, 18, 21, 30, 31, 141]. In addition, climate change adaptation measures are being pursued in various sectors, such as Korea Electric Power Corporation (KEPCO) and Imsil Nonghyup signing an MOU for safe electricity use in preparation for climate change [10], and Eurostar making a full transition to 'heatwave-specialized trains' capable of withstanding 55 degrees Celsius [46].

sections.title: 🌱 Accelerating Transition to a Carbon-Neutral Society
sections.content: Domestic efforts to transition to a carbon-neutral society are accelerating across all fronts. Chungju City, Wonju Climate Change Promotion Center, and the National Science Museum, among others, are operating climate change academies and carbon neutrality experiential education programs for elementary and middle school students, striving to foster future environmental talent [7, 9, 13, 26, 27, 28, 34, 35, 36, 37, 39, 41, 45]. Incheon City has embarked on establishing a carbon neutrality roadmap to leap forward as a 'global climate-leading city' [32, 48, 55, 65, 70, 71], and corporate sustainability efforts are also prominent, with HiteJinro publishing its '2026 Sustainability Report' and advancing its ESG strategy [38, 44, 63]. Industrial complexes are also accelerating their transformation into 'regional growth hubs' as target sites for large-scale support projects for AI and carbon neutrality transition have been confirmed [50, 56, 64, 79], and policy support is being strengthened, with the 'Greenhouse Gas Reduction Awareness Budgeting System' being fully expanded to local governments, initiated by Democratic Party lawmaker Song Jae-bong [42]. In addition, carbon neutrality efforts by various stakeholders continue, including the launch of a reduction research group for the power generation sector [57], donation of carbon neutrality point incentives [72], and the establishment of citizen-led social cooperatives [60].

sections.title: ⚡ Renewable Energy Expansion and Grid Stabilization
sections.content: Technological advancements and investments are actively underway for the stable expansion of renewable energy. Specifically, the Ministry of Climate has signed MOUs with 9 businesses, including LG Energy Solution, and is commencing the construction of the nation's first distribution grid ESS (Energy Storage System) to resolve renewable energy grid constraints in the Honam and Jeju regions, planning to connect an additional 1 GW of renewable energy to the power grid by 2030 [84, 85, 86, 88, 89, 92, 94, 95, 97, 98, 101, 102, 104, 105, 106, 108, 111, 112, 114, 117, 123, 127, 129, 137, 143, 146, 153]. Hanwha Qcells is strengthening its capabilities in the global renewable energy market by leading the construction of 'Atlas Energy Park,' a super-large renewable energy complex 22 times the size of Yeouido in Arizona, USA, achieving results across the entire process from development to EPC and sales [83, 87, 90, 91, 96, 99, 103, 107, 110, 118, 119, 120, 121, 124, 125, 126, 131, 133, 134, 138, 142, 144, 145, 149, 150]. Furthermore, various efforts for technological and market expansion are ongoing, such as the Korea Energy Agency establishing a bridgehead for domestic companies to enter the Australian offshore wind market [122, 132], Korea Hydro & Nuclear Power (KHNP) collaborating to expand domestic offshore wind power [136], and SM Electric innovating solar power generation management with an AI-based renewable energy operation platform [140]. Meanwhile, BYD has surpassed 17 million units in cumulative production of eco-friendly vehicles, setting a new global record and leading the transition to eco-friendly mobility [152].

sections.title: 🌍 International Community's Climate Response and Challenges
sections.content: The international community is exploring various response strategies amidst growing awareness of climate change, but simultaneously faces political challenges. While research on Americans' beliefs and attitudes towards climate change is underway [156], climate change is also acting as a significant variable in financial markets, with active discussions on international ETF investments focused on climate change [167]. However, political debates and challenges surrounding climate policy persist, such as former President Trump's views altering how UN agencies address the climate crisis, and a global warming skeptic being nominated as head of a national climate change office [166, 169]. In India's case, infrastructure issues during renewable energy expansion are also emerging as international challenges, with one-third of new renewable energy generation at risk of curtailment due to transmission grid constraints [100]. Amidst these complex circumstances, creative community-level adaptation efforts are also continuing, such as Ann Arbor City's revised stormwater management plan [161] and Somersworth's call for climate change-inspired downtown art installations [157].

briefing.closing: In today's briefing, we examined the severe impacts of climate change, along with domestic and international research, adaptation, and carbon neutrality transition efforts to overcome them. Responding to the climate crisis has become a necessity, not an option, and sustained attention and action are crucial. This concludes the climate briefing for July 10, 2026.

summary.phenomenon: During the June heatwave, harmful ozone concentrations continuously rose across Europe, especially Western Europe. After June 22nd, widespread air pollution was observed in the UK, and the London Mayor issued a high air pollution alert.
summary.cause: This ozone accumulation and air pollution are linked to June's record-breaking heatwave.
summary.outlook: These harmful ozone concentrations can have potentially deadly effects and are likely to increase again as the UK experiences its third heatwave of the summer.

articles.original_description: Concentrations of gas were continuously raised in Europe during June hot spell, with potentially deadly effectsWidespread air pollution was a feature of June’s record-breaking heat across western Europe and is likely to increase again as the UK experiences its third heatwave of the summer.UK weather warnings for extreme heat from 22 June were followed by a rare high air pollution alert from the London mayor.
